findings:
# Staleness Cross-Validation — SPIKE-021

**Basis:** Ring-1: research (0.88), archaeology (0.93), risk (0.88), quality (0.91)

## Staleness Matrix

| Element | Status | Criticality |
|---|---|---|
| `report_path`/`upstream_paths`/`verdict` in docs | STALE | CRITICAL |
| TIER2-PROTOCOL.md architecture | STALE-OBSOLETE | CRITICAL |
| R2 output: `write_analysis_report` vs `write_mandate_result` | STALE-CONFLICT | HIGH |
| `t3_recommendations`/`n_aspects_recommended` in schema | STALE | HIGH |
| VS Code version requirements for parallel dispatch | STALE | MEDIUM |
| `failed_approaches[]` in MandateSchema Zod | STALE | MEDIUM |
| MandateResult core schema | FRESH | — |
| Atomic write + Zod validate read/write | FRESH | — |
| Spider Web Ring 0–5 architecture | FRESH | — |
| Conventional Commits + `story/<ID>` branch | FRESH | — |

## CRITICAL-1: Phantom Fields `report_path`/`upstream_paths`/`verdict`

Four canonical docs reference these as formal exchange fields: `copilot-instructions.md` ("pass MandateResult `report_path` as `upstream_paths`"), `AGENT-DISPATCH-PROTOCOL.md` I-6 (`read_analysis_envelope(report_path)`), `TIER2-PROTOCOL.md` Phase 4 ("Return ONLY `{report_path, verdict, confidence}` to Tier-1"), and `devsteps-R0-coord.agent.md` handoffs ("Pass `report_path` from planner MandateResult").

Archaeology (conf 0.93) confirms: `schema_version: 1.0` JSON contains none of these fields. `read_mandate_results(sprint_id)` returns an array; no `report_path` exists to pass. These are informal chat-level text metaphors, not JSON schema fields. Drift propagated across 4 documents.

## CRITICAL-2: TIER2-PROTOCOL.md — Pre-Spider Web Fossil

This 183-line document describes T1→T2→T3 three-tier nesting where T2 dispatches T3 sub-agents via parallel MAP phase. `AGENT-DISPATCH-PROTOCOL.md` v4.0 (2026-03-02) states "T2 does NOT dispatch T3" and invariant I-3 forbids all non-coord `runSubagent` calls. The TIER2 frontmatter template still shows `dispatches: '[t3-agent-1]'`. Quality analyst (GAP-3, CRITICAL): a new agent reading this doc would implement illegal nested dispatch.

## HIGH-1: R2 Aspect Output Mechanism Ambiguity

All 5 R2 agent files specify `write_analysis_report` as output (caller reads via `read_analysis_envelope`). But TASK-293 archaeology shows `analyst: devsteps-R2-aspect-staleness` as a MandateResult file under `.devsteps/cbp/`. This SPIKE-021 prompt itself uses `write_mandate_result` for R2. AGENT-DISPATCH-PROTOCOL.md I-5/I-6 maintains a two-path model (analysts→MandateResult, aspects→CompressedVerdict). Live practice has collapsed to a single path.

## HIGH-2: `t3_recommendations` / `n_aspects_recommended` — Dead Documentation

`AGENT-DISPATCH-PROTOCOL.md` §2 shows coord extracting `t3_recommendations: {impact:'MUST', staleness:'MUST'}` from MandateResults to drive Round 2 aspect dispatch. Quality analyst (GAP-1, MAJOR): both fields absent from `MandateResultSchema` Zod in `cbp-mandate.ts`. Writes silently drop them. coord's data-driven Round 2 aspect selection falls back to static triage.

## MEDIUM-1: VS Code Version Requirements Absent

Research confirms: parallel subagent dispatch requires VS Code 1.109+ (Feb 2026). Session Memory `/compact` and Session Forking `/fork` require 1.110+ (released 2026-03-04). Zero version requirements documented in any agent or protocol file. `/compact`/`/fork` not mentioned as context budget tools despite immediate relevance to long-running sprints.

## MEDIUM-2: `failed_approaches[]` Convention-Only

I-9: "`failed_approaches[]` propagates through Mandate". `MandateSchema` Zod has no such field. Inbound coord→analyst mandate is free-text prompt—bypasses MCP validation. Anti-repeat relies on guide-file side-channel only.

## Community Standard Drift (Research-Informed)

| Pattern | Standard Mar 2026 | Drift |
|---|---|---|
| Natural-language Mandate | LangGraph typed state / Pydantic schemas | HIGH |
| No retry at dispatch level | LangGraph checkpointing+retry | MEDIUM |
| File-based JSON handoffs | Confirmed best practice (Hwang Feb 2026) | FRESH |
| MCP as external tool bus | Official VS Code 1.107+ standard | FRESH |
recommendations: CRITICAL: Remove report_path/upstream_paths/verdict from copilot-instructions, AGENT-DISPATCH-PROTOCOL I-6, coord handoffs, TIER2-PROTOCOL. Replace: coord reads via read_mandate_results(sprint_id).
CRITICAL: Add '> DEPRECATED (Spider Web v4.0): Non-coord agents never dispatch. See AGENT-DISPATCH-PROTOCOL.md §3.' to TIER2-PROTOCOL.md. Prevents illegal T2→T3 nested runSubagent calls.
HIGH: Resolve R2 output contract — pick one canonical path: write_mandate_result (matches live data) XOR write_analysis_report. Update all 5 R2 agent files + AGENT-DISPATCH-PROTOCOL.md I-5/I-6.
HIGH: Add t3_recommendations (Record<aspect,'MUST'|'SHOULD'|'COULD'>) + n_aspects_recommended (integer) to MandateResultSchema Zod in cbp-mandate.ts to activate data-driven Round 2 selection.
MEDIUM: Add 'requires VS Code 1.109+' to agent frontmatter; document /compact and /fork as context budget tools for coord in long-running sprints (VS Code 1.110+, released 2026-03-04).
